notes: A soldier under Dionysius, known for his fickle temper and military commands; also described as a skilled speaker and gentle man from Memphis.

docs.title: Life of Dion
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … and that in all ways he was a turbulent, fickle, and factious man, he gave way to some whom formerly he hindered when they designed to kill him, who, breaking in, murdered Heraclides in his own house. His death was much resented by the citizens.

docs.title: Letter 287
docs.author: Libanius
docs.excerpt: … carries this letter is Heraclides -- a gentle man, skilled in speaking, close to me, and an ornament to Memphis. Show me by how you treat him whether such introductions are worth making or not.

docs.title: Letter 831
docs.author: Libanius
docs.excerpt: … man of Memphis, devoted to Asclepius, gentle in character, having inherited these qualities from his father. For his father, too, was a companion of the god and in all other respects a good man — and dear to the god precisely because he was good.
